description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged and weathered surface, featuring a mix of gray, brown, and off-white tones. The stone has a lumpy, asymmetrical form with several protrusions and indentations, giving it a natural, uncut appearance. Its surface is mottled with darker patches and lighter areas, suggesting exposure to the elements and possible mineral deposits. The edges are rounded and uneven, with some areas appearing more eroded than others. There are no visible carvings or artificial modifications, and the stone appears solid and dense. Its overall look suggests it could be found in an urban park or as part of a landscaped barrier along a sidewalk.
description: A rugged, irregularly shaped stone with mottled gray, brown, and off-white colors, featuring a weathered, natural surface and rounded, uneven edges.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's broad, domed top with mottled coloration and visible surface roughness, showing two prominent side protrusions.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stone's flatter underside and more pronounced side extensions are visible, with a lighter, almost chalky appearance dominating the surface.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the stone's irregular, lumpy form and the darker, more shadowed areas on the lower right, with a clear view of the uneven edges.
description_view_3: The top-down view reveals the stone's overall footprint, showing its asymmetrical outline and a mix of light and dark patches across the surface.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: irregular mottling and patchy coloration with no artificial carvings or regular patterns
attribute_weathering: rough and eroded with visible signs of natural weathering and some discoloration
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: contrasts with paved surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements
